root_text.an8.31:1.8: ‘ahaṁ pacāmi, ime na pacanti; nārahāmi pacanto apacantānaṁ dānaṁ adātun’ti dānaṁ deti,
root_text.an8.31:1.9: ‘imaṁ me dānaṁ dadato kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchatī’ti dānaṁ deti,

translation_text.an8.31:1.3: Someone might give a gift after insulting the recipient.
translation_text.an8.31:1.4: Or they give out of fear.
translation_text.an8.31:1.5: Or they give thinking, ‘They gave to me.’
translation_text.an8.31:1.6: Or they give thinking, ‘They’ll give to me.’
translation_text.an8.31:1.7: Or they give thinking, ‘It’s good to give.’
translation_text.an8.31:1.8: Or they give thinking, ‘I cook, they don’t. It wouldn’t be right for me to not give to them.’
translation_text.an8.31:1.9: Or they give thinking, ‘By giving this gift I’ll get a good reputation.’
translation_text.an8.31:1.10: Or they give thinking, ‘This is an adornment and requisite for the mind.’
